subject: Chemistry
solution:
Aldehydes and ketones are often called by their common names instead of IUPAC names. The common names of most aldehydes are derived from the common names of the corresponding carboxylic acids  by replacing the ending –ic of acid with aldehyde. At the same time, the names reflect the Latin or Greek term for the original source of the acid or aldehyde.

The location of the substituent in the carbon chain is indicated by Greek letters α, β, γ, δ, etc. The α-carbon being the one directly linked to the aldehyde group, β carbon the next, and so on. 

In the above structure methyl group is attached to the γ  Position to the aldehyde group.It has a cyclohexane ring

γ-methyl cyclohexane carbaldehyde

The IUPAC name of the above compound is 3-Methylcyclohexanecarbaldehyde

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Mass of the organic compound (X) can be calculated as:

$\text{Mass of X} = \text{Moles} \times \text{Molar mass}$

$\text{Mass of X} = 2.0 \times 10^{-3} \text{ mol} \times 76 \text{ g mol}^{-1} = 0.152 \text{ g}$

In the Carius method for sulphur estimation, the mass of sulphur in the given mass of $\text{BaSO}_4$ is:

$\text{Mass of S} = \dfrac{32}{233} \times \text{Mass of BaSO}_4$

$\text{Mass of S} = \dfrac{32}{233} \times 0.4813 \text{ g} \approx 0.0661 \text{ g}$

The percentage of sulphur in the organic compound is given by:

$\text{\% of S} = \dfrac{\text{Mass of S}}{\text{Mass of X}} \times 100$

$\text{\% of S} = \dfrac{0.0661}{0.152} \times 100 \approx 43.487 \text{ \%}$

To express this in the requested format:

$43.487 \text{ \%} = 434.87 \times 10^{-1} \text{ \%}$

Rounding to the nearest integer, we get $435$.

Answer: $435$

subject: Chemistry
solution:
A carbocation is a molecule in which a carbon atom has a positive charge and three bonds. We can say that, basically, they are carbon cations. Formerly, it was known as carbonium ion. Carbocation today is defined as any even-electron cation that possesses a significant positive charge on the carbon atom.

The carbon cations are very reactive and unstable due to an incomplete octet. In simple words, carbocations do not have eight electrons; therefore, they do not satisfy the octet rule.

In carbocation, the hybridisation of carbon will be sp2 and its shape is trigonal planar. There is also a vacant p- orbital which indicates its electron-deficient nature. The carbon has 6 electrons in its valence shell. Due to this, it is an electron-deficient species, also known as an electrophile.

Six electron species

subject: Chemistry
solution:
A: Cation at $C_1$. $\alpha$-carbons are Methyl (3H), $C_2$ (1H), $C_6$ (2H). Total = 6.
B: Cation at $C_1$. $\alpha$-carbons are Methyl (3H), $C_2$ (2H), $C_6$ (2H). Total = 7.
C: Cation at $C_1$. $\alpha$-carbons are Methyl (3H), $C_2$ (1H), $C_6$ (2H). Total = 6.
D: Cation at $C_1$. $\alpha$-carbons are Ethyl group's $CH_2$ (2H), $C_2$ (1H), $C_6$ (2H). Total = 5.
A and C both have 6 $\alpha$-hydrogens, thus they have the same number of hyperconjugation structures.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Lassaigne's test gives presence of chlorine and nitrogen in organic compound by fusion of organic compound in the presence of sodium. The fused products in this case are:
CH3NH2.HCl→fusionNaNaCN and NaCl

NaCN gives +ve test for nitrogen and NaCl gives +ve test for halogen.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
In Dumas method, the nitrogen-containing organic compound, when heated with copper oxide in an atmosphere of carbon dioxide, yields free nitrogen in addition to carbon dioxide and water.

Traces of nitrogen oxides formed, if any, are reduced to nitrogen by passing the gaseous mixture over a heated copper gauze. The mixture of gases so produced is collected over an aqueous solution of potassium hydroxide which absorbs carbon dioxide

So, the correct answer is Copper gauze.

subject: Chemistry
options: Both Statement I and Statement II are true
Both Statement I and Statement II are false
Statement I is true but Statement II is false
Statement I is false but Statement II is true
solution:
Statement I : Sugar ($\text{C}_{12}\text{H}_{22}\text{O}_{11}$) is soluble in alcohol, whereas sodium chloride ($\text{NaCl}$) is insoluble in alcohol. Therefore, a mixture of sugar and salt can be separated based on their differential solubility in alcohol. This statement is true.

Statement II : Steam distillation is used to separate and purify organic compounds that are steam volatile and immiscible (insoluble) in water. Rose essence (essential oil) from rose petals possesses these properties, making steam distillation the appropriate method for its extraction. This statement is also true.

Therefore, both Statement I and Statement II are true.

Answer: Both Statement I and Statement II are true

subject: Chemistry
solution:
$-\mathrm{OCH}_3, \mathrm{NHCOCH}_3$ and -OH are activating groups because the atom directly bonded to benzene ring activates the ring by +R effect using its lone pair of electrons. Activating groups are ortho- and para directing groups.
-CN and $-\mathrm{SO}_3 \mathrm{H}$ are deactivating groups because the atom directly bonded to benzene is bonded to more electronegative atom through multiple bonds and they are meta directing groups due to -R effect. Halides are deactivating groups due to -I effect.

For testing of halogens, Nitric acid is added to the sodium extract because if CN- or S2- are present then they will be oxidised and removed before the test of halides.

NaCN+HNO3→NaNO3+HCN↑
Na2S+HNO3→NaNO3+H2S↑
Nilnic acid decomposed NaCN & Na2S, else they precipitate in test & misquite the resolve

subject: Chemistry
solution:
In thin layer chromatography using hexane, the least polar compound will rise to maximum height and most polar compound will rise to minimum height.

The retardation factor of each spot can be determined by dividing the distance that a particular compound has travelled by the distance between solvent front and initial spotting site. This parameter is dependent on the thin layer chromatographic plate and the solvent.

∴ Rf value for most polar compound

=25×10-2

subject: Chemistry
options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect but Statement II is correct
solution:
Statement I is correct because alkyl groups exert a +I effect (electron-donating inductive effect). This increases the electron density on the negatively charged carbon atom, destabilizing the carbanion. Thus, as the number of alkyl groups increases, the stability of the carbanion decreases. The order of stability is $\text{CH}_3^- > \text{CH}_3\text{CH}_2^- > (\text{CH}_3)_2\text{CH}^- > (\text{CH}_3)_3\text{C}^-$.

Statement II is incorrect because allyl and benzyl carbanions are highly stabilized by the resonance effect, where the negative charge is delocalized over the adjacent $\pi$ bonds or the benzene ring, rather than by the inductive effect.

Answer: Statement I is correct but Statement II is incorrect

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Aldehydic (-CHO) group has greater priority than keto group and nitro groups is always a prefix.

Root word of the given compound is hept, double bond is the primary suffix and aldehydic group becomes secondary suffix and keto and nitro groups are prefixes.

Hence, the correct IUPAC name of the given compound is

4-Methyl-6-nitro-3-oxohept-4-enal

solution: Reduced pressure distillation or vacuum distillation is used for the purification of high boiling organic liquids which decomposes at or below their boiling point.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Resonance Effect – The polarity induced in a molecule by the interaction of a lone pair of electrons with a pi bond or the interaction of two pi bonds in nearby atoms is known as the resonance effect.

The inductive and resonance effect can be effectively employed to explain the stability of carbocations, free radicals and carbanions and the acidic and basic strengths of various organic compounds.

subject: Chemistry
options: It is possible to identify each structure individually by some physical or chemical method.
All these structures are in dynamic equilibrium with each other.
Each structure exists for equal amount of time.

<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msubsup><mi>CO</mi><mn>3</mn><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>-</mo></mrow></msubsup></math> has a single structure i.e., resonance hybrid of the above three structures.
solution:
Resonating structure are hypothetical and resonance hybrid is real structure which is weighted average of all the resonating structures.

So, CO32- has a single structure i.e. resonance hybrid of the three structures.
